front: Why separate fact, interpretation, and claim?
back: Facts describe what happened, interpretations explain meaning, and claims argue what should happen politically.
A Jewish national movement that sought self-determination in the historic land, especially after persecution in Europe.
A national movement for political self-determination in Palestine, developed under late Ottoman and Mandate rule.
A UN proposal for separate Jewish and Arab states with special status for Jerusalem; accepted by Zionist leadership, rejected by Arab leadership.
The Palestinian term for the 1948 catastrophe, referring to mass displacement and loss of homes and communities.
Often described in Israel as the War of Independence and a struggle for survival after statehood declaration.
The war placed the West Bank, East Jerusalem, and Gaza under Israeli control, centralizing occupation and settlement disputes.
1990s agreements with mutual recognition between Israel and the PLO, but without final resolution of core issues.
Since 2007, Hamas has governed Gaza while Fatah-led Palestinian Authority has governed parts of the West Bank.
Refugees, Jerusalem, borders, settlements, security guarantees, sovereignty, and governance arrangements.
Treating one narrative as the only truth can hide other documented harms, fears, and political realities.
Explain causes, events, and consequences without turning the topic into a loyalty test or opinion contest.
